combined.worldview:
# Worldview

## Identity


  - "Moat is time-based: logistics, relationships, and a 5-year replication lag. Not IP-dependent."
## Settled Ground (19 claims)

- [BOULDER] "'More secure than HIPAA' is only a defensible claim with enterprise infrastructure isolation features": Scarlet explicitly frames enterprise tier as required to make the HIPAA-plus security claim defensible. Gotham may not need it on day one but transparency is warranted given clientele.
  source: Actor_Scarlet
- [BOULDER] "Claim_LocalLLM_HIPAA": Peter asserts that running LLMs locally on a practice server, disconnected from the internet, sidesteps many HIPAA compliance issues and enables richer data use.
  supports: Narrative_2
- [BOULDER] "Claim_NarrativeCharting": Peter identifies narrative-based charting — rambling about a patient for 3–5 minutes and having AI produce a consistent chart format — as a high-value AI use case.
  supports: Narrative_1
- [BOULDER] "Claim_PostStrokeEmptyMarket": 'It's good money, it's good business, it's an empty market, it's easy to pitch to doctors.'
  supports: Narrative_PostStrokeOpportunity
- [BOULDER] "Claim_TimeBasedMoat": The moat is primarily time (5-year replication lag) via logistics and relationships, not proprietary IP or protocols.
  supports: Narrative_TimeAsTheMoat
- [BOULDER] "Claim_TrainingManualGeneration": Peter envisions recording 30 hours of student training sessions and generating a citable training manual (e.g., on migraines) from the transcripts.
  supports: Narrative_3
- [BOULDER] "Claim_UWSInfiniteMarket": Peter: 'on the Upper West Side, we have infinite practitioners... and functionally infinite patients.' Scarlet added: 'functionally infinite budgets.'
  supports: Narrative_B2BReferralStrategy
- [FOUNDATION] "Claim_DoctorAuthorityLeverage": Doctors already have authority and patient relationships; getting them to prescribe acupuncture care is more effective and ethical than direct patient solicitation.
  supports: Narrative_DoctorReferralPipeline
- [FOUNDATION] "Claim_DocumentsAreStale": Scarlet: 'all documents are somewhat stale, and the goal is trying to figure out which part and which part is the direction.'
  supports: Narrative_AswrittenValueProp
- [FOUNDATION] "Enterprise Plan starts at $4,000/month and includes infrastructure isolation and compliance features": Features: single-tenant deployments, local LLM hosting, zero data retention, segmented access controls, infrastructure isolation and compliance features.
  source: Actor_Scarlet
- [FOUNDATION] "Claim_NichingIsBetterBranding": 'If you're like, I do everything, then you're kind of an expert in nothing as far as at least public perception, even if you happen to be an expert in everything, it's just bad branding.'
  supports: Narrative_NichePositioning
- [FOUNDATION] "Claim_OutcomesNotServices": Peter states explicitly: 'we are selling outcomes as opposed to services. That's one of the huge differences.'
  supports: Narrative_ManagedCareShift
- [FOUNDATION] "Claim_PelvicFloorDropout": Patients with central sensitization are likely to drop out of pelvic floor PT care; acupuncture nervous system regulation prevents dropout. Validated by Scarlet's personal experience.
  supports: Narrative_PelvicFloorPitch
- [FOUNDATION] "Claim_PricingModel": Pricing: $400/month for unlimited team members, plus consulting for knowledge extraction and document management (one-time or ongoing), plus hourly for custom ontologies and automations.
- [FOUNDATION] "Updates Claim_PricingModel: prior claim described only $400/month without distinguishing tiers": The enterprise tier and its feature boundary were not discussed explicitly on the call with Peter. This clarification was provided post-call by Scarlet.
  source: Actor_Scarlet
- [FOUNDATION] "Consulting for knowledge extraction/document management and hourly custom ontologies/automations apply to both tiers": These add-on services are tier-agnostic: one-time or ongoing consulting, and hourly for custom ontologies and automations.
  source: Actor_Scarlet
- [FOUNDATION] "Claim_SpecialistVsGeneralist": Generalist positioning actively lowers authority with specialist physicians; narrow specialty framing is required for credible doctor relationships.
  supports: Narrative_AuthorityPositioning
- [FOUNDATION] "Claim_StrongBuyIn": Peter expressed immediate, enthusiastic buy-in — seeing the usefulness of aswritten.ai immediately and stating he would love to use it.
  supports: Narrative_1
- [FOUNDATION] "Team Plan is $400/month with unlimited team members and collective memory features": Asserted by Scarlet in post-call review. Includes: unlimited team members, access to collective memory through Claude, organizational memory, knowledge extraction, content generation.
  source: Actor_Scarlet

## Open Stakes (5 claims)

- "Claim_DataSecurityPremium": Peter believes 'more secure than HIPAA' local data management is a marketable premium feature for high-net-worth and celebrity clientele.
- "Claim_DoctorRelationshipMemory": Peter identified using collective memory to track individual doctor relationship details across recorded calls, enabling personalized follow-up.
- "Claim_RevenueTarget": $3.5M gross at 70% occupancy; model validated within 18 months; expansion to additional locations at ~2 years.
- "Claim_SegmentedAccess": Peter grasped that new hires could access a segmented slice of the organizational worldview (e.g., only migraine care) from a home laptop via Claude.
- "Claim_TwoWorldviews": Scarlet identified that Gotham Acupuncture likely needs two separate worldviews: the organization as a business and the organization as a healthcare entity tracking patient treatments.

## Notions (1 emerging ideas)

- "Claim_FranchiseExpansion": Franchise-like expansion is a possibility surfaced by Scarlet; Peter has not fully committed to the model but finds it directionally aligned.

## Actors (4)

- Gotham Acupuncture — High-end managed care acupuncture practice being formed on the Upper West Side of Manhattan by Peter Caron and Jordan Barber.
- Jordan Barber — Co-founder of Gotham Acupuncture; pelvic floor specialist; former president of state acupuncture association; operates multiple insurance-based practices on Long Island.
- Peter Caron — Co-founder of Gotham Acupuncture; former VP of ASNEY; acupuncturist specializing in post-stroke recovery, digestion, and anxiety/depression; co-author of forthcoming book.
- Scarlet — aswritten.ai representative; conducted demo and discovery interview; 10 years of consulting in organizational and narrative strategy.

## Key Narratives (17)

- "Narrative_AswrittenValueProp": Differentiated from a database by understanding decisions already made, current truth, and organizational direction. Premise: all documents are somewhat stale; the goal is identifying which part reflects direction.
- "Narrative_AuthorityPositioning": Publishing a book and narrowing to defined specialties signals peer-level authority to physicians who respect specialty training.
- "Narrative_B2BReferralStrategy": 60-70% of referrals from MDs; remainder from PTs and concierge doctors. Upper West Side market characterized as having functionally infinite practitioners, patients, and budgets.
- "Narrative_DoctorReferralPipeline": B2B doctor-to-doctor approach: doctors prescribe acupuncture care, removing the ethical burden of direct patient solicitation and increasing conversion.
- "Narrative_FranchiseLikeGrowth": Apprentices gain specialties; future locations can be spun out with founder blessing, creating aligned incentives.
- "Narrative_LandAndExpand": Four core niches (post-stroke, pelvic floor, digestion, anxiety/depression) are the entry point; migraines and others are 'later sells'.
- "Narrative_ManagedCareShift": Core business model innovation: patients receive a treatment plan from a senior practitioner, then see assistants for implementation, returning to the senior for review. Increases revenue while maintaining quality.
- "Narrative_NichePositioning": Four niches: post-stroke recovery (Peter), pelvic floor care (Jordan), digestion (Peter), anxiety/depression (Peter).
- "Narrative_PelvicFloorPitch": Pitch to pelvic floor PTs: patients with central sensitization drop out of PT care; 3-5 acupuncture sessions regulate the nervous system, enabling PT completion. Win-win: PT gets paid, patient gets better.
- "Narrative_PostStrokeOpportunity": Validated protocols call for 3 sessions/week, creating strong revenue cadence. Easy to pitch to doctors. Described as 'good money, good business, empty market.'
- "Narrative_TeamBasedCare": Peter's evolving insight: best results come from teams; being part of a team means not trying to do everything the team does.
- "Narrative_TimeAsTheMoat": Honest assessment: logistics and relationships are the primary barriers, not proprietary protocols.
- "Narrative_1": The overarching narrative is that aswritten.ai can serve as a persistent, segmented organizational memory for a healthcare practice — capturing clinical knowledge, doctor relationships, training content, and business operations — while navigating HIPAA constraints through local deployment.
- "Narrative_2": Running LLMs locally on a practice server is framed both as a compliance workaround and as a differentiating premium feature for high-net-worth clientele.
- "Narrative_3": Recording training sessions, doctor calls, and clinical observations and feeding them into a memory system transforms ephemeral expertise into citable, searchable organizational knowledge.

## Hub Entities
- Narrative_1 (11 connections)
- Narrative_AswrittenValueProp (10 connections)
- Narrative_AuthorityPositioning (10 connections)
- Narrative_B2BReferralStrategy (10 connections)
- Narrative_DoctorReferralPipeline (10 connections)
- Narrative_FranchiseLikeGrowth (10 connections)
- Narrative_ManagedCareShift (10 connections)
- Narrative_NichePositioning (10 connections)

layer0.name: Core Narrative + Style
layer0.description: Mission, Vision, PositioningThesis, key actors, stakes, aggregated style

layer1.name: Essential Context
layer1.description: High-value nodes (referenced 3+ times, rich content 100+ chars, summary predicates, core-related)
layer1.useCase: Most content generation, standard AI tasks

layer2.name: Full Semantic Content
layer2.description: All domain content with full attribution. Excludes only internal pipeline bookkeeping

layer3.name: Full Context with Metadata (TriG)
layer3.description: Complete graph with named graphs per transaction. prov:wasGeneratedBy implicit in graph structure.

graph:core.name: Core + High-Value (TriG)
graph:core.description: Core concepts + high-value nodes in TriG with named graphs per transaction

graph.name: Full Graph (TriG)
graph.description: Complete graph with named graphs per transaction
graph.useCase: RDF extraction, before/after diff, full structural analysis
